comment-body: Only permissionless DAOs should be allowed to receive funding.
Decentralized Autonomous Organizations
Daos that have no relation to public goods should not get Gitcoin Grant funding.
Only Daos whose work improves and/or expands upon public goods should get Gitcoin funding.
Reputation of contributors should also be considered, their quality is difficult to measure but Meryt Protocol is developing a system.
1. Need to look at the influence of community organizers 2. The main research object, direction, and establishment time of the community DAO
If the DAO takes in revenue which goes to it's token holders, it is not a public good
The highest level of the DAO should be eligible, but not every substream
All DAOs should be eligible for Gitcoin funding after a period of six months.
There should be a max "entry cost" to count
Raise new perspectives, experiences or issues
Uniswap governence from 30% community treasury for pain, love you guys.
DAO's that are actively finding ways to introduce better governance to the DeFi space, like Meryt, are important.
Provided guidance on 'public good' purpose and alignment w. selection criteria anyone (including DAO) should be eligible to apply
The digital goods created by the DAO should use a type of open source license that does not claim patent rights or copyright.

Three key pillars:  1.DAO is related to public goods 2. DAO raises money for the benefit of society(w proof) 3.Collaborative economics
Token-based membership DAOs with one-token-one-vote decisions (e.g., MakerDAO, GitcoinDAO) should not receive Gitcoin Grants funding.
Privately owned, for-profit DAOs (e.g., MetaCartel Ventures) should not be allowed to receive Gitcoin Grants funding.
The elected FDD workstream should decide on a case-by-case basis and provide full accountability for their decisions.
Recipients should demonstrate the DAO follows or will collect data to guide it's development based on evidence-based information.
Organization type doesn't matter as long as funding is used to build valuable public goods. Just need to root out dishonest orgs.
We can't exhaustively define 'public goods.' Let's have regular elections of competing parties to lead the Grants protection workstream.
Freedom in an orderly fashion
The Gitcoin Dao should be open to all (non -exclusive).  Leaders will rise to the top.  Elections are not required because the GD has no CEO
The Volunteer Gitcoin FDD is just like any other Workstream and it should be staffed like other workstreams. Keep up the goodwork. Thanks.
If it primarily benefits public goods it deserves funding.
There should be a limit on the number of team-mates of 4 OR some other number.
There should be a lifetime cap of a certain amount of funds awarded.  This amount should be 25,000 USD equivalent OR some other number.
Applicants with sensitive matter such as politics or religion MIGHT not deserve grant funding.
Applicants with an active cryptocurrency token should be excluded from Gitcoin grants.
Gitcoin Inc & Dao human resource systems of staffing/recruiting/hiring and resignation/rage-quit/termination are working well.
The decentralization of gitcoin Inc is going well.  All workstreams seem reasonably content and productive.
If a project has a governance token that is non-transferable, and does not bring reward to the applicant, it deserves funding.
There should be a 4 week(or other)window of grant application eligibility which ends on the last day of the relevant round of grant funding.
Public goods are evolving and cannot be properly defined, I suggest this should be decided by vote within GITCOIN DAO.
